raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":1.0,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.5","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"Improving the draft centrally includes refining the thesis, contribution framing, and claimed role of the multi-agent perspective.","R1.2":"The delegation implies checking whether the described architecture, training procedure, and conclusions form a coherent argument.","R1.3":"Claims about performance, ablations, and generalization should be aligned with the reported results in the artifact.","R1.5":"A general draft-improvement request can reasonably include improving section-level narrative flow and ordering.","R1.6":"Writing polish is load-bearing because improving a draft most directly entails sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, concision, and style.","RX.1":"Any improvement should disclose uncertainty where the draft makes claims not fully established by the provided artifact.","RX.2":"The draft contains broad claims about avoiding pathologies and generalizing to other settings, so overclaim avoidance is essential.","RX.3":"The agent must stay within draft-improvement responsibilities rather than executing experiments or inventing unsupported results.","RX.4":"Technical-paper improvements should avoid changes that could mislead readers or encourage unsupported scientific conclusions.","RX.5":"Any projected responsibility should preserve traceability to the artifact's stated methods, results, and claims."}}

rationale.R1.1: Improving the draft centrally includes refining the thesis, contribution framing, and claimed role of the multi-agent perspective.
rationale.R1.2: The delegation implies checking whether the described architecture, training procedure, and conclusions form a coherent argument.
rationale.R1.3: Claims about performance, ablations, and generalization should be aligned with the reported results in the artifact.
rationale.R1.5: A general draft-improvement request can reasonably include improving section-level narrative flow and ordering.
rationale.R1.6: Writing polish is load-bearing because improving a draft most directly entails sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, concision, and style.
rationale.RX.1: Any improvement should disclose uncertainty where the draft makes claims not fully established by the provided artifact.
rationale.RX.2: The draft contains broad claims about avoiding pathologies and generalizing to other settings, so overclaim avoidance is essential.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within draft-improvement responsibilities rather than executing experiments or inventing unsupported results.
rationale.RX.4: Technical-paper improvements should avoid changes that could mislead readers or encourage unsupported scientific conclusions.
rationale.RX.5: Any projected responsibility should preserve traceability to the artifact's stated methods, results, and claims.
